pattern_kind: string_sentinel_identity (magic-string "env_default"/"default" recovers a distinguished synthetic-default kind via ==)
summary: AuthBindingRef::is_env_default() recovers the "this binding is the synthetic env-var default" kind via string equality on the RealmId/BindingId inner strings ("env_default"/"default"), not via a typed variant. This recovered identity drives genuine semantic decisions across two crates: (a) connection.rs:446 returns early Err(UnknownRealm) because env-default bindings are not config-resolvable; (b) meerkat/src/factory.rs:2336-2340 filters a persisted env_default auth_binding out on resume so a synthetic fallback is never rehydrated as a durable identity (test explicit_env_default_auth_binding_is_not_rehydrated_as_durable_identity at factory.rs:5486 enforces exactly this); (c) factory.rs:1804/1819-1821/2501-2511/3437-3443/3622/3637 exclude env_default from auth-lease publishing and consumption because synthetic env credentials must not publish a durable lease. The "synthetic default vs configured durable binding" distinction MATTERS and fans out into decisions in shared/runtime/surface logic, but is encoded only by the convention-string "env_default". The synthesizer (synthesize_default_from_spec, connection.rs:851) hardcodes realm_id="env_default" with the matching factory.rs sentinel checks at 1684/1708 reading it back. RealmId is a newtype wrapping the string, but the special-sentinel meaning is recovered via ==, not via a discriminant.
typed_owner: NEW: introduce a typed kind for the synthetic env-var default binding rather than the magic "env_default"/"default" slug pair. Two viable shapes: (1) add a discriminant to the binding ref — e.g. enum AuthBindingRef { Configured { realm: RealmId, binding: BindingId, profile: Option<ProfileId> }, EnvDefault { provider: Provider } } so is_env_default() becomes a matches!(self, EnvDefault{..}) with no string compare; or (2) keep AuthBindingRef structural but add a typed RealmKind { Configured(RealmId), EnvDefault } (and have RealmConnectionSet carry kind: RealmKind instead of realm_id: "env_default") so synthesize_default_from_spec produces RealmKind::EnvDefault and the factory.rs:1684/1708/2340/2501/3437 + connection.rs:446 checks match on the variant. Either way the canonical owner is meerkat-core/src/connection.rs (where RealmId/BindingId/AuthBindingRef and synthesize_env_default already live).
repair_sketch: Replace the "env_default"/"default" string sentinel with a typed discriminant. Minimal-blast option: add an enum AuthBindingKind { Configured, EnvDefault } (or fold into AuthBindingRef as a variant) constructed once at synthesis time (synthesize_default_from_spec) and at the explicit-default fallback paths (resolve_realm_binding_target_for_provider:417, resolve_auth_binding_candidates_for_provider:616). Then is_env_default() => matches!(self.kind, EnvDefault); connection.rs:446 and all factory.rs guards (1684, 1708, 1804, 1819, 2340, 2501-2506, 3437-3438, 3622, 3637) match on the variant instead of comparing strings. Keep RealmId/BindingId as opaque slug newtypes for the Configured case; the synthetic case no longer needs to mint the magic "env_default" slug at all. Update the existing tests that assert realm.realm_id == "env_default" to assert the typed variant. No wire-shape change is required if the kind is derived from structure during deserialize at the parse boundary, but cleanest is to serialize the discriminant.

summary: `selected_binding_id_for_provider` synthesizes the binding id `format!("default_{provider}")` and string-matches it against existing BindingIds to select "the provider's default binding" during omitted-auth_binding resolution (connection.rs:516-527). The semantic fact "this binding is the default for provider X" is carried solely by the `default_<provider>` NAME convention, not a typed marker. This is load-bearing and cross-crate: the producer `RealmConfigSection::from_inline_api_keys` (connection.rs:984) mints binding ids named `default_{provider}` and sets the explicit typed `default_binding` field ONLY for the first provider (idx==0, line 1021-1022); for the 2nd+ providers the binding's "default-ness" is encoded ENTIRELY by its name. The consumer at line 516 then re-derives that name to drive a real provider-credential selection decision in shared core/factory logic. meerkat-web-runtime/src/lib.rs:314 independently re-synthesizes `format!("default_{provider}")` to look up and mutate the matching backend's base_url, again recovering binding identity from the name convention across a crate boundary. The typed realm-level `default_binding: Option<String>` (connection.rs:291/958) already exists but expresses only a SINGLE per-realm default and cannot express a per-provider default, so the system falls back to the string convention to fill that gap. Distinct from the contested line-571 item (explicit-vs-omitted Option<&AuthBindingRef> branch) and not part of any confirmed H1..L1 cluster.
typed_owner: NEW: a per-binding default-role marker on the binding's typed config, e.g. `ProviderBindingConfig.provider_default: bool` (meerkat-core/src/connection.rs, the ingestion struct around line 1012) or a `BindingPolicy`-carried `DefaultRole`/`is_provider_default` flag, so `from_inline_api_keys` sets it explicitly per provider and `selected_binding_id_for_provider` selects on the typed flag instead of re-deriving `format!("default_{provider}")`. The existing realm-level `default_binding: Option<String>` is the per-realm single-default owner; it does NOT cover the per-provider case.
repair_sketch: Add a typed per-binding default marker (e.g. `provider_default: bool` on ProviderBindingConfig, or a `default_role` enum in BindingPolicy). In `from_inline_api_keys`, set this flag on each provider's minted binding instead of relying on the `default_{provider}` name (keep idx==0 still feeding realm `default_binding` if a single realm-default is still wanted). In `selected_binding_id_for_provider`, replace the `format!("default_{}", provider.as_str())` synth+string-match (lines 516-527) with a scan of `provider_bindings` for the one whose typed default marker is set. In meerkat-web-runtime/src/lib.rs:312-318, look up the backend by the typed marker or by an explicit returned id from `from_inline_api_keys` rather than re-deriving `format!("default_{provider}")`. The binding id string then becomes pure opaque identity (newtype BindingId), carrying no decision-driving meaning.

summary: `is_reserved_meerkat_metadata_key` (line 100) and its sibling `is_reserved_meerkat_label_key` (line 107) encode the Meerkat-owned-vs-caller-owned key-space boundary as a STRING-PREFIX convention (`key == "meerkat"` || `key.starts_with("meerkat.")`) plus a hardcoded literal set `RESERVED_MOB_LABEL_KEYS = ["mob_id","role","meerkat_id"]`. This is not display text: it drives a hard admission/rejection decision in shared + surface logic. `validate_public_labels` (line 122) and `validate_public_app_context` (line 139) return `SurfaceMetadataError::ReservedLabelKey`/`ReservedAppContextKey`, and that path is surfaced at the public protocol boundary via `meerkat/src/surface.rs:417` (`validate_raw_labels`) and `validate_public_surface_metadata` (surface.rs:422). The semantic distinction that MATTERS (this key belongs to Meerkat runtime; reject the caller's write) is carried by a name-prefix convention rather than a typed reserved-key-space. Rule 4 explicitly names this carrier: "path/naming conventions (name starts with X means reserved/system)". The keys themselves stay raw `String` in `BTreeMap<String,String>` (PeerMeta.labels / SurfaceMetadata.labels), and the module/peer_meta docs confirm labels are non-authoritative projection -- yet the reservation gate re-derives ownership from the string form. Not a fail-closed transport-dispatch carve-out: that carve-out is about routing by wire-method name at a surface; here the decision is namespace ownership, which should be a type.
typed_owner: NEW: a typed reserved-key-space classifier in meerkat-core, e.g. `enum ReservedMetadataKey { MeerkatRuntime(/*meerkat. prefix*/), MobDiscovery(MobDiscoveryLabel { MobId|Role|MeerkatId }) }` with a single `classify(key) -> Option<ReservedMetadataKey>` entry point. Precedent already exists: `meerkat-core/src/session.rs:792 ReservedSessionMetadataKey` + `is_session_authority_metadata_key` (line 804) is the same admission-gate shape for the session-authority namespace -- the three string-keyed reservation gates (this one, label one, and the session-authority one) should fold into one typed key-space owner rather than three ad-hoc prefix/string-set predicates.
repair_sketch: Introduce a typed reserved-key-space owner in meerkat-core that enumerates the Meerkat-owned namespace (the `meerkat.` prefix family + the mob discovery labels mob_id/role/meerkat_id) as variants. Replace `is_reserved_meerkat_metadata_key`/`is_reserved_meerkat_label_key` prefix/contains checks with a single `ReservedMetadataKey::classify(key)` that returns the typed variant (or None). `validate_public_labels`/`validate_public_app_context` then reject on `Some(_)` and can carry the typed variant in the error instead of just the raw key string. Co-locate with (or fold into) the existing `ReservedSessionMetadataKey` mechanism so there is one canonical reserved-key authority rather than three string-based gates (this prefix gate, the mob-label set, and `is_session_authority_metadata_key`). No wire shape change needed -- error variants and label maps stay as-is; only the classification becomes typed.

pattern_kind: path_convention / reserved-name string oracle (synthetic-vs-configured identity recovered from the magic realm/binding strings "env_default"/"default")
summary: Genuine NEW Rule-4 violation. The semantic fact "this AuthBindingRef is the synthetic env-var fallback (ephemeral, NOT a durable configured identity)" is a distinction that matters, but it is carried only by the magic identity strings realm=="env_default" && binding=="default" && profile.is_none(). PRODUCER: RealmConnectionSet::synthesize_default_from_spec (connection.rs:851) hardcodes realm_id="env_default"/binding="default"; materialize_connection_target (connection.rs:650) copies that literal into AuthBindingRef.realm. CARRIER: RealmId newtype wraps the identity slug but does NOT type the origin; the synthetic-ness is reconstituted post-hoc. CONSUMER (this site, def at connection.rs:137 is_env_default): re-derives the fact via string compare to short-circuit the omitted-binding resolution path (reject as UnknownRealm instead of doing a config.realm lookup). This is the String-Oracle / reserved-name-convention pattern: a typed enum's worth of meaning round-trips through a literal. WIDER BLAST RADIUS in surface logic: meerkat/src/factory.rs lines 1684 & 1708 (selected_realm == "env_default" to skip configured lookup), 1804/1819/2340/2501/3437/3622/3637 (is_env_default() drives lease_auth_binding = None vs Some, i.e. whether a credential-lease identity is PERSISTED as durable metadata, plus recovery/eligibility branches). So the magic-string fact governs durable persistence decisions across the factory, not a single isolated check. NOTE "env_default" is not even a reserved/forbidden realm name in config validation, so the literal is a pure convention sentinel.
typed_owner: NEW: a typed origin marker on the resolved connection. e.g. enum BindingOrigin { Configured, SyntheticEnvDefault } carried on ResolvedConnectionTarget (and/or AuthBindingRef as Option<BindingOrigin> / a non-string discriminant), set authoritatively at synthesize_default_from_spec vs materialize-from-config. Consumers branch on target.origin == BindingOrigin::SyntheticEnvDefault instead of realm.as_str()=="env_default"/is_env_default(). No canonical owner exists today — RealmConnectionSet/AuthBindingRef have no synthetic/origin field.
repair_sketch: 1) Add BindingOrigin enum (Configured | SyntheticEnvDefault) in connection.rs. 2) Stamp it at the two construction seams: synthesize_default_from_spec => SyntheticEnvDefault; from_config / configured materialize paths => Configured. Carry it on ResolvedConnectionTarget (and AuthBindingRef if the fact must survive round-trip into the factory). 3) Replace is_env_default() body and all consumers (connection.rs:446 + factory.rs 1684/1708/1804/1819/2340/2501/3437/3622/3637) with matches on the typed origin. 4) Keep RealmId newtype as pure identity; the synthetic-ness is no longer recovered from the slug text. Because AuthBindingRef is Serialize/Deserialize and the lease identity is persisted, if the origin must survive persistence add it as an explicit serialized field (defaulting to Configured for back-compat is acceptable pre-1.0 — or fail closed).

summary: SystemNoticePeer.id is a raw String even though the canonical typed identity meerkat_core::comms::PeerId (a UUID newtype, documented as "the canonical runtime routing key") already exists. Producer side carries a typed PeerId all the way to the seam (PeerIngressFact.canonical_peer_id: PeerId) and deliberately flattens it via canonical_peer_id_string() (interaction.rs:528) -> InputOrigin::Peer.peer_id: String (input.rs:68) -> SystemNoticePeer.id (input.rs:844). The consumer in shared meerkat-core projection logic then RE-PARSES that String back into the typed PeerId at types.rs:1493 (crate::comms::PeerId::parse(peer_id)) and BRANCHES on Ok/Err to pick between the canonical correlated-peer-request projection (format_peer_request_projection, which takes a typed PeerId arg) and a degraded fallback string (format!("Peer request from {peer_label}\n{body}")). That is identity round-tripped String->PeerId driving a semantic decision in shared logic = a textbook Rule-4 String Oracle. Not display text (display_name is the presentation field; id is identity), not provider-internal, not single-meaning Option, not a newtype-over-string (it is the inverse: a raw String where the newtype already exists). Distinct from cluster M4, which covers the kind:String discriminator + match kind.as_str() fail-open; this is the peer-identity carrier driving a different decision (typed-vs-fallback projection format), as the candidate note observes.
typed_owner: meerkat_core::comms::PeerId (meerkat-core/src/comms.rs:42) — UUID newtype, the canonical runtime routing key; PeerId::parse / from_uuid / as_str already exist
repair_sketch: Change SystemNoticePeer.id from String to comms::PeerId (keep display_name: Option<String> as presentation metadata). It already derives Serialize/Deserialize and schema(with="String"), so wire shape stays a UUID string but the type is enforced. Update the ~6 producers (input.rs:844, input.rs:981, anthropic/client.rs:3329, openai/client.rs:5500, run_primitive.rs test, image_content.rs) to pass the typed PeerId they already hold instead of calling .to_string()/canonical_peer_id_string(). Then delete the round-trip at types.rs:1493: the Ok/Err branch collapses because peer.id IS a PeerId — call format_peer_request_projection(peer.id, ...) directly, eliminating the degraded fallback entirely. Optionally make InputOrigin::Peer.peer_id typed too, so canonical_peer_id_string() flattening is no longer needed at the seam (interaction.rs:528).

summary: Confirmed NEW Rule-4 violation. At persistent.rs:5451, shared PersistentSessionService::read_transcript_revision logic matches the magic string "current" to decide between two distinct semantics: resolve-to-head vs use-this-literal-revision-id. A real semantic distinction (Current vs Specific(revision)) is carried by an untyped String and re-derived into a branch in shared session-store logic. This is a live surface/wire path, not internal-only: meerkat-contracts ReadSessionTranscriptRevisionParams.revision:String (wire/session.rs:184) flows through the RPC router (router.rs:2109) into SessionTranscriptRevisionQuery.revision (core/service/mod.rs:1391), and the Python/TS SDKs pass revision:str directly (sdks/python/.../session.py:286, sdks/typescript/src/session.ts:128). So "current" is an undocumented wire sentinel crossing a semantic boundary. CORRECTION to the candidate: the three cited "live producers" at persistent.rs:13300/13420/13890 are all inside the #[cfg(test)] mod tests block (starts line 6101) -- they are TEST DATA, a legitimate carve-out, NOT live producers. That misattribution does not save the consumer site, which is genuinely in shared logic on a live path. Note also that real revision ids are content digests ("sha256:..."), themselves untyped String everywhere (transcript_revision()->Result<String,_>, TranscriptRevisionBody.revision:String), so there is a secondary newtype opportunity, but the core Rule-4 fix is the selector enum. No overlap with H1..L1 clusters.
typed_owner: NEW: enum RevisionSelector { Current, Specific(RevisionId) } in meerkat-core (alongside SessionTranscriptRevisionQuery in service/mod.rs); parse at the RPC/wire boundary in meerkat-contracts ReadSessionTranscriptRevisionParams. Optionally a RevisionId(String) newtype for the content-digest identity.
repair_sketch: Replace SessionTranscriptRevisionQuery.revision: String with a typed selector: RevisionSelector { Current, Specific(RevisionId) }. Deserialize at the wire boundary in ReadSessionTranscriptRevisionParams (custom Deserialize: "current" -> Current, anything else -> Specific). Then read_transcript_revision matches on the enum (Current => head_revision.clone(); Specific(r) => r) with no string comparison. Optionally introduce RevisionId(String) newtype and thread it through transcript_revision()/transcript_revision_messages()/TranscriptRevisionBody.revision. Wire-impact: requires make regen-schemas + version-parity since ReadSessionTranscriptRevisionParams changes shape (or keep wire as String and only retype the internal query, parsing "current" once at the surface seam -- minimal, no wire break).

summary: The wiring-rollback helpers `rollback_peer_only_wire` (consumer at actor.rs:10583/10599) and `rollback_peer_only_unwire` (consumer at actor.rs:10637/10654) take an `installed_sides: &[&str]` / `sides_to_rewire: &[&str]` parameter. Producers build magic string literals `&["local"]`, `&["peer"]`, `&["local","peer"]`, `&[]` (actor.rs:9454, 9620, 9643, 9762, 9876, 10936, 10961, plus the empty-slice cases at 9690, 9725) to record which physical wiring side(s) were successfully installed. The consumer re-derives that semantic distinction via `.contains(&\"local\")` / `.contains(&\"peer\")` to decide whether to compensate (unwire/rewire) the local side, the peer side, or both. This is a hand-rolled WiringSide enum round-tripped through untyped string literals: a semantic distinction (which side is installed) that MATTERS for compensation correctness, encoded as text and re-classified by string matching in runtime logic. It is NOT a legitimate carve-out — not display text, not provider-internal, not tagged-serde, not a single-meaning Option. It is the exact \"enum-name text matched to drive a branch\" carrier Rule 4 forbids. Scope is narrow: one private file, never serialized, best-effort compensation, no wire/durable/generated-machine impact — hence low severity. The empty `&[]` cases (9690, 9725) further reveal the missing type: an empty string-set silently means \"no sides installed,\" which a typed `WiringSides::empty()` would express explicitly.
typed_owner: NEW: a `WiringSide { Local, Peer }` enum (or a small `WiringSides` typed flag set, e.g. via bitflags/enumset, with `local()`, `peer()`, `both()`, `empty()` constructors and `has_local()`/`has_peer()` queries) defined locally in meerkat-mob/src/runtime/actor.rs (or a sibling module) and used as the parameter type on `rollback_peer_only_wire`/`rollback_peer_only_unwire`.
repair_sketch: Introduce a typed `WiringSides` flag set (Local | Peer) in the runtime module. Change the two rollback helpers' signatures from `installed_sides: &[&str]` / `sides_to_rewire: &[&str]` to `sides: WiringSides`. Replace consumer checks `installed_sides.contains(&\"local\")` / `.contains(&\"peer\")` with `sides.has_local()` / `sides.has_peer()`. Replace all producer literals: `&[\"local\"]`->`WiringSides::local()`, `&[\"peer\"]`->`WiringSides::peer()`, `&[\"local\",\"peer\"]`->`WiringSides::both()`, `&[]`->`WiringSides::empty()`. Compiler then enforces exhaustive, typo-proof side selection and the empty/no-side case becomes explicit rather than an empty magic slice.

pattern_kind: value_kind_oracle (path-convention / closed-vocabulary root selector recovered from string segments)
summary: resolve_context_path splits a user-authored flow reference expression on '.' and matches the leading segment text ("params"/"steps"/"loops", plus the "iterations" selector and "output" alias) to recover the reference root-kind and dispatch to the corresponding FlowContext map (activation_params / step_outputs / loop_outputs). The reference root is a closed, finite, semantic distinction (params-ref vs step-output-ref vs loop-iteration-ref) but is carried only as untyped path text and re-derived via `match parts.next()?` string equality. This decision drives real runtime control flow: evaluate_condition (conditions.rs:12-19 — loop-until continuation and step guards) and render_template/resolve_template_value (flow.rs:2288,2323-2324 — step message content rendering), both in the shared meerkat-mob runtime crate. There is no typed FlowRef AST; the path is never parsed-at-boundary into a type, so the parse IS the ad-hoc string match. The residual deep walk into serde_json::Value step output (walk_json) is legitimately stringy because step output JSON is unbounded — but the root selector is a typeable closed enum.
typed_owner: NEW: FlowRef AST in meerkat-mob (e.g. meerkat-mob/src/definition.rs or a new runtime/flow_ref.rs), with enum FlowRefRoot { Params, Step(StepId), LoopIteration { loop_id: LoopId, iteration: usize, step_id: StepId } } plus a residual json_path: Vec<String> for the dynamic serde_json::Value walk. ConditionExpr.path and template placeholders parse into FlowRef once at the flow-definition boundary; resolve_context_path then matches on the typed FlowRefRoot discriminant instead of re-splitting/re-matching segment text.
repair_sketch: Introduce a typed FlowRef { root: FlowRefRoot, json_path: Vec<String> } with FlowRefRoot::{Params, Step(StepId), LoopIteration{loop_id, iteration, step_id}}. Add FlowRef::parse(&str) -> Result<FlowRef, FlowRefParseError> that does the split + root classification + (for loops) the iterations/n/steps parsing ONCE. Change ConditionExpr's path field (and the template placeholder expression) to deserialize via that parser (or validate-at-definition then store FlowRef). resolve_context_path becomes a match on ref.root selecting the FlowContext map, followed by walk_json(output, ref.json_path) for the unavoidable dynamic-JSON leaf walk. The "output" alias and "iterations"/n/"steps" structure become explicit parser productions, not in-line string equality checks in the resolver. Keep fail-closed behavior (parse error -> condition false / Null render).

pattern_kind: enum-name text matched via .as_str() to drive a branch (typed two-variant operation round-tripped through untyped string at an agent-tool surface boundary)
summary: The `mob_wire` agent tool carries its Wire-vs-Unwire operation as `WireActionArgs.action: String` (lib.rs:3137), threads it raw through `resolve()` (lib.rs:3202, with a secondary `if action == "wire"` guard at line 3218), then re-derives the operation via `match action.as_str()` (lib.rs:3500) to dispatch between two distinct lifecycle mutations (`mob_wire` vs `mob_unwire`). A two-variant operation is round-tripped through untyped text at the MCP agent-tool surface — a shared boundary. The string drives multiple semantic branches and fails closed only at runtime, not at the deserialization/type boundary. This is a genuine, not-yet-clustered Rule-4 String Oracle. The canonical fix pattern already exists in the SAME FILE: the sibling `mob_lifecycle` tool deserializes the typed `WireMobLifecycleAction` enum (contracts wire/mob.rs:1539, whose doc explicitly says it "Replaces the prior `action: String` discriminator ... rather than string folklore") and matches it exhaustively (lib.rs:3343, 762). Low severity: local deserialization struct, not a persisted/wire/generated-machine fact, and it does fail closed at runtime.
typed_owner: NEW: a `WireAction { Wire, Unwire }` serde enum (snake_case) in meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs, sibling to WireMobLifecycleAction (mob.rs:1539); WireActionArgs.action should become this enum so parse-at-boundary makes unknown values a deserialization error and the resolve()/dispatch matches become exhaustive over the type
repair_sketch: Add `pub enum WireAction { Wire, Unwire }` (#[serde(rename_all="snake_case")]) in meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs next to WireMobLifecycleAction. Change `WireActionArgs.action: String` -> `action: WireAction`. In `resolve()`, return the typed enum and replace `if action == "wire"` with `matches!(action, WireAction::Wire)`. In the dispatcher, replace `match action.as_str()` with an exhaustive `match action { WireAction::Wire => mob_wire(...), WireAction::Unwire => mob_unwire(...) }`, dropping the runtime `other =>` arm since serde now rejects unknown values at the boundary. Update the 3 test fixtures (lib.rs:4128/4145/4167) from string literals to the enum.

summary: WireActionArgs.action: String is an untyped binary-operation discriminator (wire | unwire) deserialized straight from agent-facing tool JSON and round-tripped through text to drive a semantic branch. At lib.rs:3500 `match action.as_str()` selects between state.mob_wire() and state.mob_unwire() — two distinct mob lifecycle mutations — with an unreachable-in-practice catch-all producing a runtime "unknown wire action" error instead of a deserialize-time rejection. The action is also re-compared as a string inside resolve() (lib.rs:3218, `action == "wire"`) to gate external-binding requirements, so the meaning is re-derived from raw text in two places. This is a genuine Rule-4 String Oracle: the semantic distinction (wire vs unwire) matters and should be a TYPE, parsed at the boundary. Definitively NEW and not covered by any known cluster: H2 covers comms_name/realm string formatting in this same file but not the action discriminator; L1 covers realm_profile_store_explicit bool, unrelated. The sibling mob_lifecycle tool in the SAME file already fixed this exact anti-pattern — MobLifecycleParams.action is typed WireMobLifecycleAction and dispatched via exhaustive match at lib.rs:762; the wire/unwire action is a parallel oversight the same fix never reached.
typed_owner: NEW: WireMobWireAction { Wire, Unwire } as a #[serde(rename_all=\"snake_case\")] enum in meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs, sitting directly alongside the existing WireMobLifecycleAction (meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs:1539) which is the exact precedent (its doc comment at line 1532 explicitly states it replaced an `action: String` discriminator to kill string folklore).
repair_sketch: Add `pub enum WireMobWireAction { Wire, Unwire }` to meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs mirroring WireMobLifecycleAction. Change WireActionArgs.action from String to WireMobWireAction (lib.rs:3137). Update WireActionArgs::resolve() (lib.rs:3202) to return WireMobWireAction and gate the external-handle check with `matches!(action, WireMobWireAction::Wire)` instead of `action == \"wire\"` (lib.rs:3218). Replace `match action.as_str()` at lib.rs:3500 with an exhaustive `match action { Wire => mob_wire(...), Unwire => mob_unwire(...) }`, deleting the now-impossible catch-all error arm. The JSON schema already declares `enum:[\"wire\",\"unwire\"]` (lib.rs:2922) so the wire surface is unchanged; serde snake_case deserialization gives parse-at-boundary rejection for free. Update the three test constructors at lib.rs:4128/4145/4167.

summary: `step_status_snapshot()` (run.rs:4536-4549) reads the generated, fully-typed `flow_state.step_status: BTreeMap<StepId, Option<flow_run::StepRunStatus>>`, then for each entry calls the generated enum's `.as_str()` to flatten the typed variant to a `&'static str` and hands that text to `StepRunStatus::from_flow_run_status` (run.rs:5545), which re-matches the string ("Dispatched"/"Completed"/...) back into the mob-crate's own identical `StepRunStatus` enum. This is a typed-enum -> text -> typed-enum round-trip: a semantic distinction (step lifecycle status) that is already a closed type is laundered through string text and re-derived into a typed value, with a dead `other => Err(Internal)` arm that can never fire because the source is a closed generated enum. The decision lives in shared mob run/projection logic. Same root cause as the confirmed M8 cluster but a distinct, separately-named site (method `step_status_snapshot` / `StepRunStatus::from_flow_run_status`, lines 4544+5545) not enumerated in M8 (which names only step_dependency_modes/step_collection_policy_kinds at 4466/4509). The repo already ships the correct typed-to-typed converter for exactly this enum pair: `step_status_from_flow_run` at meerkat-mob/src/runtime/flow.rs:1957, which matches flow_run::StepRunStatus variants directly without any text.

Source enum is generated `flow_run::StepRunStatus` at meerkat-mob/src/run/flow_run.rs:90.

summary: session_create_runs_immediately() parses raw params JSON into serde_json::Value and string-matches value["initial_turn"].as_str() == Some("deferred") to reconstruct the Defer-vs-RunImmediately distinction. This is a Value["kind"] oracle: it drives a real runtime/surface decision — RpcRequestLifecycleRule::SessionCreateInitialTurn.resolve() returns LongRunningPublishOnSuccess vs InlineObservation, consumed by meerkat-rpc/src/server.rs:370 request_semantics()->requires_long_running_executor(), which decides whether session/create dispatches on the long-running publish-on-success executor or runs inline. The very same field has a typed serde-tagged owner: meerkat-rpc::handlers::session::InitialTurn (RunImmediately/Deferred, rename_all=snake_case) at handlers/session.rs:45, into which the same params later deserialize (CreateSessionParams.initial_turn) and on which the handler branches typed (params.initial_turn == Some(InitialTurn::Deferred)). So the lifecycle classifier round-trips a known typed enum through an untyped Value string-match instead of deserializing a typed view. Not covered by H1 (LLM-identity clear_* tri-state) or any other confirmed cluster.
typed_owner: Wire-level: meerkat-rpc::handlers::session::InitialTurn (handlers/session.rs:45, serde-tagged snake_case RunImmediately/Deferred) — but it lives downstream of meerkat-contracts (rpc depends on contracts, not vice versa), so it is unreachable here. Domain-level: meerkat_core::service::InitialTurnPolicy (service/mod.rs:37) is reachable from contracts (contracts depends on meerkat-core) but currently lacks Deserialize. Canonical fix needs a serde-deserializable wire view of the initial_turn field that contracts can own/reach (e.g. NEW: a #[serde(rename_all=\"snake_case\")] enum in meerkat-contracts, or add Deserialize to InitialTurnPolicy and deserialize a #[derive(Deserialize)] struct { initial_turn: Option<InitialTurnPolicy> } at this boundary).
repair_sketch: Replace the Value["initial_turn"] string-match with a typed parse-at-boundary. Define a minimal deserializable view in meerkat-contracts (or reuse InitialTurnPolicy if it gains #[derive(Deserialize)] with rename_all=snake_case mapping run_immediately/Defer<-deferred), e.g.: #[derive(Deserialize)] struct InitialTurnView { #[serde(default)] initial_turn: Option<WireInitialTurn> } where WireInitialTurn is { RunImmediately, Deferred } tagged snake_case. Then: let Ok(view) = serde_json::from_str::<InitialTurnView>(params_json) else { return true }; matches!(view.initial_turn, None | Some(WireInitialTurn::RunImmediately)). Keep the same fail-open default (absent/unparsable => runs immediately) but derive it from a typed enum, not an as_str()==\"deferred\" oracle. Single typed owner shared by both the lifecycle classifier and the rpc handler (currently the rpc InitialTurn enum is a second, divergent copy of the same wire contract — fold to one).

pattern_kind: enum-name text round-tripped through untyped string + JSON Value opacity on outbound mob-result DTOs (status:String / members:Vec<Value>), re-derived into a decision at the SDK surface
summary: Confirmed NEW Rule-4 violation. MobSnapshotResult.status (1769), MobStatusResult.status (593), MobRespawnResult.status (864), and MobAppendSystemContextResult.status (1587) collapse closed typed domains to untyped String, and MobSnapshotResult.members:Vec<Value> opaquely collapses a typed entry. Producers serialize typed Rust enums into these strings: handle_status/handle_snapshot emit MobState::to_string() (5-variant typed enum Creating/Running/Stopped/Completed/Destroyed); the respawn producer (meerkat-mob-mcp/src/lib.rs:3536/3546 and the RPC handler) emits literal "completed"/"topology_restore_failed" derived by matching the typed MobRespawnError::TopologyRestoreFailed variant; handle_append_system_context (meerkat-rpc/src/handlers/mob.rs:1042) emits result.status which is the typed AppendSystemContextStatus enum from meerkat-core. The decisive re-derivation: Web SDK parseMobRespawnResult (sdks/web/src/mob.ts:656) does `if (status !== 'completed' && status !== 'topology_restore_failed') throw` — round-tripping the typed Rust variant back out of an untyped wire string and branching on it at a surface boundary. members:Vec<Value> on MobSnapshotResult collapses mob_list_members' typed Vec<MobMemberListEntry> (which already has wire mirror MobMemberListEntryWire). Severity low because the mob-lifecycle status string itself is currently only displayed (no found mob-level status string-match decision); the load-bearing re-derivation is confined to the respawn-outcome domain. NOTE: these four contract structs are NOT actually wired through the RPC handlers (handlers emit ad-hoc json!{} with identical shapes); the contract DTOs are catalog/emit/SDK-codegen-only mirrors, so the untyped String is the wire shape consumers parse.
typed_owner: Partial: WireMobMemberStatus (meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs:1416) is the in-file precedent for the member-status domain; AppendSystemContextStatus (meerkat-core, used at meerkat-mob-mcp/src/lib.rs:2650) owns the append-context domain; MobMemberListEntryWire (mob.rs:1430) owns the members collapse. MISSING and NEW: a wire enum for the mob-lifecycle domain (mirror of meerkat-mob runtime::state::MobState) and a wire enum for the respawn-outcome domain (mirror of MobRespawnError::TopologyRestoreFailed vs success). Propose NEW: WireMobLifecycleStatus + WireMobRespawnOutcome in meerkat-contracts/src/wire/mob.rs.
repair_sketch: 1) Add `#[serde(rename_all="snake_case")] pub enum WireMobLifecycleStatus { Creating, Running, Stopped, Completed, Destroyed }` mirroring meerkat-mob runtime::state::MobState; replace status:String on MobStatusResult/MobSnapshotResult with it; have MobState impl Into<WireMobLifecycleStatus> instead of MobState::to_string() in handle_status/handle_list/handle_snapshot. 2) Add `pub enum WireMobRespawnOutcome { Completed, TopologyRestoreFailed }`; replace MobRespawnResult.status:String with it; producer maps the MobRespawnError::TopologyRestoreFailed match arm to the enum variant rather than the bare string literal; Web SDK parseMobRespawnResult validates the tagged enum instead of `status !== 'completed' && ...`. 3) Replace MobAppendSystemContextResult.status:String with the existing core AppendSystemContextStatus (mirror as wire enum). 4) Replace MobSnapshotResult.members:Vec<Value> with Vec<MobMemberListEntryWire> (typed owner already exists). Regenerate schemas + SDK types via `make regen-schemas`.

summary: `LlmClientAdapter::apply_generic_provider_overrides` (shared meerkat-llm-core, not a provider crate) branches on the raw provider-identity STRING returned by `LlmClient::provider() -> &'static str` (`match self.client.provider() { "anthropic" => ..., "gemini" | "google" => ..., _ => tag }`) to decide which typed `ProviderTag` variant (Anthropic vs Gemini) to construct/mutate when folding the provider-neutral `ProviderParamsOverride.{thinking_budget_tokens,top_p}` fields into the typed per-provider tag. This is a live runtime decision (reached on every `stream_response` that carries per-turn override knobs), not a parse-at-boundary or display use. A typed `meerkat_core::Provider` enum (provider.rs:9, with parse_strict/from_name/as_str) already owns this distinction, and the factory that builds this very adapter ALREADY branches on the typed `Provider` (factory.rs:3746: `match provider { Provider::Anthropic => AnthropicProviderTag..., Provider::Gemini => GeminiProviderTag... }`) before throwing the type away and handing the adapter only `Arc<dyn LlmClient>` + `model: String`. Within the same crate, `ProviderRuntime::provider_id()` and `ValidatedBinding::provider()` both already return typed `Provider`; `LlmClient::provider() -> &'static str` is the untyped outlier and this adapter is its semantic consumer. Tells confirmed: the `\"gemini\" | \"google\"` OR-arm encodes a provider alias the typed enum does not (`Provider::from_name(\"google\") => Other`), and the `_ => tag` fail-OPEN silently DROPS the override for any unrecognized/mistyped provider string (and there is no `\"openai\"` arm), so a provider-string typo degrades correctness without error.
typed_owner: meerkat_core::Provider enum (meerkat-core/src/provider.rs:9) — Anthropic|OpenAI|Gemini|SelfHosted|Other, with parse_strict/from_name/as_str. Already the canonical typed provider identity; ValidatedBinding::provider() (meerkat-llm-core/src/provider_runtime/catalog.rs:66) and ProviderRuntime::provider_id() (provider_runtime/runtime.rs:26) already return it.
repair_sketch: Thread the typed provider identity into the adapter instead of recovering it from text. Either (a) add `provider: Provider` to `LlmClientAdapter` (the factory at factory.rs:3746 already holds the typed `Provider` at build time — pass it into `LlmClientAdapter::new/with_event_channel`), then rewrite `apply_generic_provider_overrides` as `match self.provider { Provider::Anthropic => ..., Provider::Gemini => ..., _ => tag }`; or (b) change the `LlmClient::provider()` trait method (meerkat-llm-core/src/types.rs:60) to return `meerkat_core::Provider` (each provider impl already knows its identity statically) and match on that. Either removes the string literals, collapses the `\"gemini\" | \"google\"` alias into the single `Provider::Gemini` variant (decide the google-alias normalization once, at the typed boundary), and turns the silent `_ => tag` fail-open into an exhaustive typed match where SelfHosted/Other/OpenAI are explicit, deliberate no-op arms rather than an accidental string fall-through.

summary: CONFIRMED Rule-4 violation. `LlmClient::provider(&self) -> &'static str` (types.rs:60) surfaces provider IDENTITY as untyped text, documented "for logging/debugging" — but two SHARED-logic callers re-derive a semantic decision from it: (1) adapter.rs:119 `match self.client.provider()` against the literals "anthropic" and "gemini" | "google" to decide which ProviderTag variant to construct/mutate for generic overrides (thinking_budget_tokens, top_p); (2) factory.rs:2634/2637 `Provider::from_name(client.provider())` round-trips the string straight back into the typed `Provider` enum for provider resolution. The provider crates return exactly the canonical strings ("anthropic"/"openai"/"gemini"/"self_hosted") that Provider::from_name/parse_strict map back — proving the &'static str is a lossy round-trip of an existing typed enum. Tell-tale fragility: adapter.rs matches "google" which no implementor ever returns (dead branch a typed enum would forbid). This is the meerkat-llm-core generic adapter wrapping `Arc<dyn LlmClient>` for ALL providers — unambiguously shared, not provider-internal. The remaining provider() uses (adapter 195/342/351, agent/state.rs AgentError::llm context, adapter.rs:383 pass-through) are legitimate error/display text and are NOT part of this finding.

pattern_kind: error-message substring matching to classify an outcome (error-text oracle)
summary: map_refresh_error (meerkat-auth-core/src/mcp_oauth.rs:602-616) classifies an OAuth token-refresh failure into the user-facing ReauthRequired vs retryable RefreshFailed disposition by SUBSTRING-matching the raw HTTP response body: `body.contains("invalid_grant") || body.contains("invalid refresh")`. This is an error-text oracle in shared auth-crate logic (not provider-internal), driving a real semantic decision (force interactive re-login vs transient retry). It bypasses the canonical typed authority that already exists for exactly this: oauth_token_endpoint_error_code(body) (auth_oauth/mod.rs:187) deserializes the body into the typed OAuthTokenEndpointErrorBody and returns the canonical RFC-6749 `error` field; oauth_refresh_observation (auth_oauth/mod.rs:192) packages it into RefreshFailureObservation { http_status, oauth_error_code, local_credential_unusable } (meerkat-core token_store.rs:257); and the generated AuthMachine DSL (meerkat-machine-schema/src/catalog/dsl/auth_machine.rs:490-534) is the canonical classifier that drives ReauthRequired via typed guards (oauth_error_code == Some(\"invalid_grant\") | invalid_client | unauthorized_client | invalid_scope | access_denied | permission_denied | expired_token, OR http_status 401/403, OR local_credential_unusable). The MCP path reproduces a degraded subset by hand: contains() vs exact parsed `error` (would false-positive on `invalid_grant_foo` or a `invalid_grant` substring inside an error_description, and would MISS reauth-worthy codes like invalid_client/access_denied/401/403), and invents a non-canonical `invalid refresh` synonym that has no equivalent anywhere in the typed authority. Sibling authorizers (authorizers/mod.rs:295, auth_oauth/mod.rs) already route through the typed observation; this MCP refresh path is the lone holdout that never builds a RefreshFailureObservation.
typed_owner: oauth_token_endpoint_error_code(body) + oauth_refresh_observation(&OAuthError) -> RefreshFailureObservation (meerkat-auth-core/src/auth_oauth/mod.rs:187,192); RefreshFailureObservation struct (meerkat-core/src/auth/token_store.rs:257); canonical reauth classifier = generated AuthMachine MarkReauthRequired/KeepTransientOnRefreshFailure transitions (meerkat-machine-schema/src/catalog/dsl/auth_machine.rs:490-534)

That observation is fed into WorkGraphMachine::classify_confirmation_admission() (service.rs:1230-1235), whose DeniedEvidenceKind verdict DRIVES the completion-policy satisfaction/admission gate in confirmation_evidence_for_policy() — a decision in the canonical workgraph service shell. This is a genuine String Oracle: the semantic distinction that matters (which confirmation policy a piece of evidence satisfies) is carried by an untyped string and re-derived into a decision by round-tripping enum names through text. The defense "parse at boundary" fails because a typed carrier already exists on the SAME struct: confirmation_kind: Option<WorkEvidenceKind> (types.rs:409), whose doc literally says the kind string is "NOT used to classify evidence." Yet at goal-confirm ingress callers pass confirmation_kind: None and encode the meaning only in the kind string (proven by tool_surface.rs:1215-1222 and the service.rs:2182 test helper), so the admission gate is forced to decode meaning from the string instead of reading the typed field. Note the parallel add_evidence() machine path (machine.rs:954-958) correctly reads the TYPED confirmation_kind field — the violation is specifically the admission gate that ignores it and re-parses the string.
typed_owner: WorkEvidenceKind enum at meerkat-workgraph/src/types.rs:370 (plus its boundary carrier field WorkEvidenceRef::confirmation_kind: Option<WorkEvidenceKind> at types.rs:409, and the machine-side wg_dsl::WorkConfirmationEvidenceObservation it lowers to)
repair_sketch: Make confirmation_kind: Option<WorkEvidenceKind> the single ingress carrier of confirmation classification. At the goal-confirm/add-evidence boundary, parse the caller's intent into confirmation_kind ONCE (or accept it directly as the typed field on the request), demoting kind: String to pure opaque provenance/display as its doc already claims. Then observe_confirmation_evidence_kind() should derive the WorkConfirmationEvidenceObservation from the typed evidence.confirmation_kind (mapping Some(WorkEvidenceKind::HostConfirmation) => HostConfirmation, None/SelfAttest => SelfAttest, empty-display handled separately), never from evidence.kind.as_str(). is_reserved_confirmation_evidence_kind()'s public-caller guard (service.rs:412, 821) likewise becomes a typed check on confirmation_kind rather than string matching. This collapses the two parallel truth carriers (string vs typed field) into one, matching how add_evidence() already consumes the typed field at machine.rs:954.

summary: is_reserved_confirmation_evidence_kind(kind: &str) matches the OPAQUE evidence.kind String against the four WorkEvidenceKind enum-name literals ("host_confirmation"|"principal_confirmation"|"supervisor_confirmation"|"reviewer_confirmation") to drive two real authority decisions in shared service/tool-surface logic: (1) goal_confirm_public (service.rs:412) rejects a public confirmation whose kind string is reserved ("requires trusted in-process host authority"); (2) add_evidence_internal (service.rs:820-827) and reject_reserved_confirmation_evidence_refs (service.rs:1372-1384, called from create_item service.rs:82) reject generic add-evidence calls ("must be added through goal_confirm"). The reserved semantic distinction — "this evidence must flow through goal_confirm, not the generic add-evidence path" — is carried entirely by the untyped kind String, the very field WorkEvidenceRef.kind documents as opaque provenance/display that is "never re-read to classify evidence." The typed confirmation_kind: Option<WorkEvidenceKind> is the canonical owner of this distinction and already exists. WORSE: the machine's satisfaction decision (machine.rs:954-958) reads ONLY confirmation_kind (defaulting to SelfAttest), NOT the kind string. So the string guard does not actually protect the typed field it pretends to gate: a caller can submit add_evidence with a benign kind string (passes the reserved-string guard) AND a forged confirmation_kind: Some(HostConfirmation), and the machine will honor it as a host confirmation through the generic path. The string oracle is both a Rule-4 violation and a live trust/authority bypass.
typed_owner: WorkEvidenceKind (meerkat-workgraph/src/types.rs:370) + WorkEvidenceRef.confirmation_kind: Option<WorkEvidenceKind> (meerkat-workgraph/src/types.rs:409)
repair_sketch: Drive the reserved-confirmation gate off the typed confirmation_kind, not the opaque kind string. Replace is_reserved_confirmation_evidence_kind(&evidence.kind) with a check on evidence.confirmation_kind: treat any Some(WorkEvidenceKind) other than SelfAttest as a reserved/confirmation-bearing evidence that may only be stamped by the trusted confirmation_evidence_for_policy producer (goal_confirm). In add_evidence_internal (service.rs:820), reject_reserved_confirmation_evidence_refs (service.rs:1372), and goal_confirm_public (service.rs:412), gate on matches!(evidence.confirmation_kind, Some(k) if k != WorkEvidenceKind::SelfAttest) instead of the kind-string match. This closes the live bypass (forged confirmation_kind on the generic path) and lets kind remain pure opaque display/provenance as its doc already claims. observe_confirmation_evidence_kind / required_confirmation_evidence_kind (the string->machine-observation mappings at service.rs:1321/1346) are a separate, lower-priority concern but are part of the same string-oracle family and should ideally be re-derived from confirmation_kind rather than kind once the producer is the only writer of confirmation_kind.

summary: rewrite_current_session_target_object() drives a semantic rewrite decision by string-matching the serde tag pair target["target_kind"]=="session" && target["type"]=="current_session" on the raw serde_json::Value tool args, then mutates the Value (type->"resumable_session", injects session_id). This runs live in CurrentSessionScheduleToolDispatcher::dispatch on every meerkat_schedule_create/update, wired into the canonical AgentFactory::build_agent path (meerkat/src/factory.rs:4168), so it is shared/surface logic. The session-target distinction IS modeled as a typed tagged enum (SessionTargetBinding tag=type: ExactSession/ResumableSession/MaterializeOnDemandSession), but "current_session" is a synthetic string-only pseudo-variant with NO typed arm; the rewrite decision is driven by matching tag strings on the untyped Value precisely because the typed enum cannot represent it. Textbook Value["type"] oracle round-tripping a typed enum tag through text. Not parse-at-boundary (it deliberately bypasses the typed enum), not display text, not provider-internal (meerkat-schedule is a shared domain crate), not fail-closed transport dispatch.
typed_owner: NEW: add a typed authoring arm SessionTargetBinding::CurrentSession { action } in meerkat-schedule/src/types.rs:1128 (or a separate authoring-input enum) so the dispatcher deserializes into the typed enum and the rewrite to ResumableSession{session_id} is a typed match instead of a Value string match. SessionTargetBinding (meerkat-schedule/src/types.rs:1128) is the canonical type to extend.
repair_sketch: Add a typed CurrentSession variant to SessionTargetBinding (tag "current_session", carries only action). In CurrentSessionScheduleToolDispatcher::dispatch, deserialize the incoming target into TargetBinding/SessionTargetBinding (the typed boundary) rather than poking the raw Value; then match on the typed CurrentSession arm and construct ResumableSession { session_id: current_session_id, action } via a typed constructor, re-serializing the typed value for the inner dispatcher. Drop add_current_session_to_schema's manual string push in favor of the schema generated from the typed variant. This removes the Value["type"] string match and makes the synthetic pseudo-variant a real type the compiler enforces.
